summary: Iter 95 — HR Letters + Bonus Registers (Forms A/B/C/D) + Annual Returns (ER & ISMW). Backend: 17/17 pytest tests passed (templates for all 4 letter types, letter CRUD + PDF, bonus financials upsert+get, all 4 bonus form PDFs, ER+ISMW annual return PDFs, bonus-run preview total_bonus=~727k). Frontend: fixed a double-JSON-stringify bug in hr-letters.tsx and bonus-registers.tsx that caused a 422 Pydantic error on save; after fix, full end-to-end save+PDF works. HR Letters: Kankani firm picked, appointment type selected, employee code 50 chosen, Load Template auto-filled subject + body + preview letterhead, edit + Save & Download returned 200 with Ref no KEPS/APT/2026/001, Letter Register tab lists the new letter with PDF + delete buttons. Bonus Registers: financials saved (PUT 200), all 4 Form PDFs (A/B/C/D) + ER + ISMW returned 200 application/pdf. Regression: /salary-day-sheet loads with Kankani firm, shows HH:MM Duty HRS + OT columns + Show button + Day Salary rupee amounts; /attendance-grid has view-inout-salary testID + OT/salary columns visible.

ui_bugs.issue: body was double-serialized (JSON.stringify passed into api() which JSON.stringify's again), causing every save to fail with 422 Pydantic 'valid dictionary or object' error. Fix applied.
same double-JSON-stringify bug on PUT /api/admin/bonus-registers/financials. Fix applied.

action_items: Please review the two frontend fixes in /app/frontend/app/hr-letters.tsx (saveLetter) and /app/frontend/app/bonus-registers.tsx (saveFin) — I replaced `body: JSON.stringify({...})` with `body: {...}` to match the codebase's api-client convention (client already stringifies).
Consider having api() client be defensive: if body is a string that looks like JSON, do not re-stringify. This would prevent this bug pattern from recurring on future PUT/POST callers.
Optional: seed a couple of employees with `gender` and non-Rajasthan `address` so the Equal-Remuneration / ISMW annual return PDFs show non-trivial rows for demo screenshots.
critical_code_review_comments: hr_letters.py + statutory_registers.py are cleanly written, well-commented, use `_s()` for latin-1 sanitisation of PDF text, and correctly exclude MongoDB `_id`.
Role scoping (`_check`, `_check_scope`) is enforced consistently for super_admin / sub_admin / company_admin, with company_admin blocked from Form C/D (annual return) by design.
Ref-No generation uses count-based sequence (`count_documents + 1`) which is race-prone under concurrent saves; consider atomic $inc on a counter document. Not blocking.

success_rate.frontend: 100% after fix (all target flows verified: HR Letters generate + register + PDF, Bonus Forms A-D, ER, ISMW, salary-day-sheet, attendance-grid)

seed_data_creation: Left 1 test HR letter behind (Ref KEPS/APT/2026/001, appointment for emp code 50 SURENDRA SINGH). It can be deleted from the Letter Register UI. Bonus financials saved for cmp_527fecdd7c FY 2025-26 (gross_profit 5,000,000; dep 200k; dev 100k; tax 300k; other 50k; allocable 60%; 2 set-on rows).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The api() wrapper in /app/frontend/src/api/client.ts line 108 auto-stringifies body — callers must pass a plain object, never a pre-stringified string. Two Iter-95 screens (hr-letters, bonus-registers) had this bug; fixed. If you add more admin write endpoints, follow the `body: {...}` convention.
rca_of_issue: RCA: hr-letters.tsx line 213 and bonus-registers.tsx line 122 wrote `body: JSON.stringify({...})`. The shared api() client at src/api/client.ts:108 does `body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined`, so the payload was serialized twice, arriving at FastAPI as a JSON-encoded string. Pydantic then produced a 422 with `model_attributes_type` complaining that a string was passed where a dict was expected. Reproduction: pick firm, load template, click Save & Download → 422 in devtools with a giant Pydantic error banner. Mitigation applied: replace `JSON.stringify(...)` with `{...}` in both call sites. Verified end-to-end: save returns 200 with ref_no and letter appears in the register.
